story_engine: Political intrigue and supernatural threats converge as multiple noble houses vie for power while ancient dangers awaken, forcing characters to choose between honor and survival.

plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: Expose the truth about Joffrey's illegitimate claim and ensure rightful succession
plotlines.obstacle: The Lannisters' willingness to kill to protect their secret
plotlines.stakes: The realm falls under illegitimate rule and war consumes Westeros

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Power
plotlines.goal: Transform from powerless exile into a queen capable of reclaiming her birthright
plotlines.obstacle: Her brother's control, Dothraki customs, and her own inexperience
plotlines.stakes: The Targaryen line dies out and she remains a victim forever

plotlines.name: Robb: Justice
plotlines.goal: Rescue his father and defend the North against Lannister aggression
plotlines.obstacle: The Lannisters' superior resources and political maneuvering
plotlines.stakes: The Stark family is destroyed and the North falls under southern control

plotlines.name: Jon: Duty
plotlines.goal: Find his place and purpose in the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: His privileged background and the growing supernatural threat
plotlines.stakes: He abandons his vows and the realm loses a defender against the coming darkness

plotlines.name: Arya: Freedom
plotlines.goal: Escape King's Landing and survive the chaos following her father's execution
plotlines.obstacle: Being hunted by the Lannisters while disguised and alone
plotlines.stakes: She is captured and killed, ending her chance for revenge

plotlines.name: Sansa: Survival
plotlines.goal: Survive as a hostage in King's Landing while maintaining hope
plotlines.obstacle: Joffrey's cruelty and her complete powerlessness
plotlines.stakes: She breaks under torture or is killed for her family's rebellion

plotlines.name: Night's Watch: Defense
plotlines.goal: Investigate and prepare for the supernatural threat beyond the Wall
plotlines.obstacle: Ancient enemies thought to be myths returning with unknown power
plotlines.stakes: The realm is overrun by an unstoppable supernatural force

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern beyond the Wall, then a White Walker kills two rangers while Will flees
Ned Stark executes deserter Will for abandoning the Night's Watch, bringing his sons to witness justice
The Stark children find direwolf pups and each claims one, with Jon taking the albino runt Ghost
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters, visiting Lyanna's crypt and asking Ned to be Hand of the King
Robert proposes marriage between Prince Joffrey and Sansa Stark to unite their houses
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo in exchange for a Dothraki army to reclaim the Iron Throne
Jorah Mormont presents himself to Daenerys at her wedding, offering books and beginning his service
Catelyn receives Lysa's secret letter claiming the Lannisters murdered Jon Arryn, transforming Robert's offer into urgent duty
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace while climbing, and Jaime pushes him from the tower to protect their secret
Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he survives, while Catelyn refuses to leave his bedside
Jaime expresses concern to Cersei about what Bran might remember, but Cersei dismisses the threat
Jon gives Arya the sword Needle and says farewell to unconscious Bran before leaving for the Night's Watch
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya, joining the royal party
Joffrey threatens the butcher's boy Mycah with his sword during a riverside encounter
Arya's direwolf Nymeria bites Joffrey to protect Mycah, then Arya drives Nymeria away
Robert orders Sansa's direwolf Lady executed when Cersei demands justice and Nymeria cannot be found
The Hound hunts down and kills the innocent butcher's boy Mycah
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself physically with Drogo
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal rather than a victim, beginning to shift their marriage dynamic
An assassin with a Valyrian steel dagger attempts to kill the comatose Bran
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed until Bran's direwolf kills the attacker
The assassination attempt convinces Catelyn the Lannisters are trying to silence Bran, so she resolves to ride south to warn Ned
Jon Snow arrives at Castle Black and discovers most Night's Watch recruits are criminals and outcasts, not noble defenders
Jon dominates in combat training against other recruits, earning their resentment instead of respect
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son and help the weaker recruits instead
Jon begins helping the weaker boys train, earning their acceptance
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council, meeting Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to the Lannisters and Iron Bank due to Robert's reckless spending
Robert demands a lavish tournament in Ned's honor despite the Crown's debt, overruling Ned's objections
Catelyn arrives secretly in King's Landing and meets with Ned through Littlefinger's arrangement
Littlefinger identifies the Valyrian steel dagger as one he lost to Tyrion Lannister in a bet, directing suspicion toward the Lannisters
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north to prepare Winterfell while he investigates from King's Landing
Arya overhears two shadowy figures discussing Stark-Lannister tensions and the need to delay war until their plans are ready
Arya fails to convince Ned of the significance of what she overheard in the castle cellars
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot, defying Viserys's authority
When Viserys strikes Daenerys, her Dothraki guards intervene and humiliate him at her command
Viserys realizes with shock that Daenerys is no longer under his control and the Dothraki respect her, not him
Ned visits the blacksmith's apprentice Gendry and discovers he has black hair and resembles young Robert Baratheon
Ned obtains the genealogical book Jon Arryn was studying before his death
Tyrion arrives at Winterfell and gives Bran designs for a special saddle to help him ride despite his paralysis
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, showing suspicion of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys privately that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation in Westeros
Drogo declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World when Daenerys announces her pregnancy
Viserys grows increasingly bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
Ser Gregor Clegane kills Ser Hugh of the Vale during the tournament joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of how Gregor burned his brother the Hound's face as a child
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was not accidental and that someone is eliminating Jon Arryn's witnesses
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion at the Crossroads Inn and calls on her father's bannermen to arrest him for Bran's attempted murder
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie instead of south to King's Landing to avoid Lannister pursuit
Robert receives word that Daenerys is pregnant and orders her assassination despite Ned's protests about murdering a young woman and unborn child
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest over the assassination order and announces he will return to Winterfell
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target and reveals that Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the high road to the Eyrie, and Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive
Catelyn reaches the Eyrie where her sister Lysa imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells after already convicting him in her mind
Jon Snow is assigned to the stewards rather than rangers, which he considers an insult until Sam explains he's being groomed for command
Arya continues sword training with Syrio Forel while Ned watches with growing concern about the coming war
Littlefinger takes Ned to a brothel to see another of Robert's bastards, deepening Ned's understanding of Jon Arryn's investigation
Ned realizes that all of Cersei's children are golden-haired while all Baratheon children have black hair, discovering they are products of Cersei and Jaime's incest
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets with armed men, kills Ned's guards, and personally wounds Ned before demanding Tyrion's release
Ned wakes from his injuries and Robert demands he resume his duties as Hand, threatening to give the position to Jaime if Ned refuses
Villagers petition the court reporting Ser Gregor Clegane's raids in the Riverlands, and Ned recognizes this as Lannister provocation
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice and summons Tywin Lannister to answer for his bannerman's crimes
Tyrion demands trial by combat at the Eyrie rather than face Lysa's biased judgment
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen in trial by combat using speed and cunning, securing Tyrion's freedom
Viserys draws his sword in Vaes Dothrak and threatens Daenerys, demanding his crown from Drogo
Drogo kills Viserys by pouring molten gold over his head, and Daenerys declares he was no dragon because fire cannot kill a dragon
Jon and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Ghost emerges from the forest carrying a severed human hand, alerting the Watch that the dead are stirring
Ned studies the genealogy book and confirms that every Baratheon lord has produced black-haired children, holding the secret that could destroy the Lannisters
Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's bastards and giving her a chance to flee before Robert returns
Cersei coldly refuses Ned's mercy, declaring that in the game of thrones you win or you die
Robert Baratheon is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during his hunt, having been too drunk to defend himself
On his deathbed, Robert names Ned Protector of the Realm, but Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in the will
Robert dies and Ned sends a letter to Stannis at Dragonstone informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty to enforce the rightful succession
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey, but Ned refuses to compromise
Renly offers Ned backing to seize Joffrey before the Lannisters can act, but Ned refuses to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing after Ned rejects his offer
A wine merchant attempts to poison Daenerys, but Jorah intervenes after being warned by Varys, exposing the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to cross the Narrow Sea and place his unborn son on the Iron Throne
Two dead rangers are brought inside Castle Black, their corpses reanimating as wights during the night
Jon fights a wight that attacks the Lord Commander, saving his life by setting the creature on fire
Ned enters the throne room with Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm, expecting City Watch support
Cersei tears up Robert's will and declares Joffrey king, rejecting Ned's claim
Littlefinger signals the gold cloaks who turn on Ned's men, killing his household guard and putting a dagger to Ned's throat
Ned is arrested and imprisoned in the Red Keep dungeons
Sansa is kept under Lannister guard in her chambers, unaware of the full betrayal
Syrio Forel fights Lannister soldiers with a wooden sword to help Arya escape the purge
Arya recovers Needle and flees into the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the Westeros invasion, with Daenerys horrified by the violence
Daenerys defies Dothraki custom by protecting captive women from rape, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights and kills his bloodrider Mago in single combat to support Daenerys, but takes a chest wound
Daenerys accepts help from captive healer Mirri Maz Duur to treat Drogo's wound
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Valyrian steel sword Longclaw for saving his life
Jon learns of Ned's arrest and attempts to desert the Night's Watch to join Robb's war
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his Night's Watch vows and return
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes with promises of Tywin's gold and weapons instead of fighting them
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where his father is assembling an army to march against the Riverlands
Robb calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men to rescue Ned
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry a Frey daughter and Arya will marry a Frey son
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin while leading the main army to Riverrun
Tyrion fights in the Battle of the Green Fork and is knocked unconscious as Tywin wins the feint battle
Robb's forces ambush and defeat Jaime's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood, capturing Jaime
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess treason to save Sansa's life
Ned agrees to lie and confess for his daughter's sake, abandoning his honor
Ned publicly confesses to treason at the Great Sept of Baelor before the crowd
Joffrey defies expectations and orders Ned's execution instead of showing mercy
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with Ice as Sansa screams and Arya is pulled away by Yoren
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection threatening his life
Daenerys asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo, who warns only death pays for life
During the blood magic ritual, Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent
Daenerys wakes to discover Drogo is alive but in a vegetative state, and her son was stillborn and monstrous, the price of Mirri Maz Duur's blood magic
Mirri Maz Duur reveals she deliberately destroyed Drogo's mind as revenge for her village's destruction, telling Daenerys she saved no one
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal, leaving her with only a small band of loyal followers
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at Ned's severed head mounted on a spike, and has a guard strike her when she shows defiance
The Hound stops Sansa from pushing Joffrey from the rampart and quietly wipes blood from her lip
Arya travels north disguised as a boy named Arry with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits, including Gendry who is unknowingly fleeing Lannister hunters
Northern lords reject serving any Baratheon king, and Greatjon Umber declares Robb the King in the North as all the lords kneel and take up the chant
Catelyn visits the captive Jaime and strikes him in grief and rage for what the Lannisters have done to her family
Lord Commander Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch and announces a great ranging to find Benjen and investigate the White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest beyond the Wall
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo, places her dragon eggs on it, and ties Mirri Maz Duur to the flames as sacrifice
Daenerys walks into the fire herself over Jorah's protests, emerging unburned at dawn with three living dragons - the first in over a century
